phrases.text: ProofPay proves what an autonomous agent paid for.
This is a prerecorded evidence walkthrough — documenting a real, successful Hedera testnet payment.
The payment was completed on July twenty-first, twenty twenty-six.
No new payment executes during playback.
A blockchain transaction proves that value moved between accounts.
But it does not prove what an A P I delivered.
ProofPay closes that gap.
It binds the payment to the request, evidence, output, provenance, and software version.
Here is the complete flow.
Codex requests evidence.
The resource server returns H T T P four oh two.
Codex signs the exact H bar transfer.
Blocky four oh two verifies the transfer and sponsors the network fee.
Hedera settles.
The evidence unlocks.
And ProofPay issues a signed receipt.
This slide reconstructs the completed purchase from the committed proof bundle.
The buyer authorized one million and one tinybar to the seller.
The facilitator paid the network fee.
The exact public account identifiers and transaction are shown on screen.
No signing key is shown.
Hedera's sponsored-fee model separates transfer authorization from network fees.
The buyer signs the exact value transfer.
Blocky four oh two validates it, adds the fee-payer signature, and submits it.
ProofPay releases nothing until settlement succeeds.
For this transaction, ProofPay verified the buyer debit, seller credit, exact amount, network, consensus result, and transaction identifier.
Only then did it unlock deterministic DeltaSignal evidence from the Strategy ten K filing.
The E D twenty-five five nineteen receipt binds the payment, delivery, evidence, and provenance.
This is the completed Hedera testnet transaction.
The full transaction identifier is shown on screen and linked in the repository.
HashScan and the Hedera mirror node independently confirm the buyer, seller, fee payer, amount, successful result, and consensus time.
HashScan proves that value moved.
The portable verifier proves what that value unlocked.
Sixteen independent checks validate the trusted signer, quote, payment terms, request, encrypted payload, artifact, evidence, source date, output, and settlement.
All sixteen checks passed.
ProofPay fails closed.
Change one field in the delivered evidence, and verification rejects the bundle.
The signed artifact and evidence hashes no longer match.
Mutation is detected immediately.
For DeltaSignal, this creates pay-per-evidence infrastructure for autonomous agents.
Buyers pay only for the research they need.
Each delivery is auditable, portable across payment rails, and backed by public settlement proof.
Thank you for watching.
The repository contains the performed payment bundle and the public transaction link.
The complete transcript and verifier reproduce every evidence check.
ProofPay — verifiable payment for verifiable evidence.
